title: pericopsis angolensis subsp. laxiflora
intro: Pericopsis angolensis subsp. laxiflora, also known as the African white cedar, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, and to alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of P. angolensis subsp. laxiflora typically involve infusions or decoctions made from the leaves or bark, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits.
